title: TRMF Integration with Enterprise Risk Management
verbatim: A financial institution must ensure that the TRMF is an integral part of the financial institution’s enterprise risk management framework (ERM).
translation: A financial institution must ensure that its Technology Risk Management Framework is embedded as an integral part of the institution's broader enterprise risk management framework, not operated as a standalone process.
evidence: Framework alignment with enterprise risk management

artifacts.name: Technology Risk Management Framework
artifacts.description: Comprehensive framework document covering governance, assessment, and reporting

artifacts.name: Board Approval Record
artifacts.description: Evidence of board approval of the technology risk management framework

artifacts.name: Framework Review Report
artifacts.description: Annual review report on the effectiveness and currency of the framework

title: TRMF Minimum Requirements
verbatim: The TRMF must include the following: (a) clear definition of technology risk; (b) clear responsibilities assigned for the management of technology risk at different levels and across functions, with appropriate governance and reporting arrangements; (c) the identification of technology risks to which the financial institution is exposed, including risks from the adoption of new or emerging technology (refer Appendix 9 on Guidance on Emerging Technologies); (d) risk classification of all information assets / systems based on its criticality; (e) risk measurement and assessment approaches and methodologies; (f) risk controls and mitigations; (g) continuous monitoring to timely detect and address any material risks; (h) effective information system to ensure the technology risk profile remains accurate and up to date; (i) identification of key resources and interdependencies (including critical third party service providers and their connected parties) which support delivery of critical technology functions; (j) undertake scenario analysis to strengthen capacity and readiness to resume critical systems under severe conditions; and (k) effective incident management policies and procedures to minimise the impact of any service disruption on the financial institution and its customers, by restoring the affected service or system to a secure and stable state as quickly as possible.
translation: The Technology Risk Management Framework must cover a comprehensive set of elements including a clear definition of technology risk, assigned responsibilities across functions, identification of technology risks (including from emerging technologies), risk classification of all information assets and systems, risk measurement methodologies, controls and mitigations, continuous monitoring, an accurate information system for the risk profile, identification of critical resources and third-party interdependencies, scenario analysis for critical system recovery under severe conditions, and effective incident management policies to minimise service disruption impact.
evidence: Risk assessment schedule and completion records
Evidence of assessment upon significant change events

artifacts.name: Risk Assessment Methodology
artifacts.description: Documented methodology for identifying and assessing technology risks

artifacts.name: Technology Risk Assessment Report
artifacts.description: Completed risk assessment report covering all critical assets

artifacts.name: Risk Assessment Schedule
artifacts.description: Schedule showing planned and completed risk assessments

title: Independent Technology Risk Management Function
verbatim: A financial institution must establish an independent enterprise-wide technology risk management function which is responsible for– (a) implementing the TRMF and CRF; (b) advising on critical technology projects and ensuring critical issues that may have an impact on the financial institution’s risk tolerance are adequately deliberated or escalated in a timely manner; and (c) providing independent views to the board and senior management on third party assessments, where necessary.
translation: A financial institution must establish an independent, enterprise-wide technology risk management function. That function is responsible for implementing the Technology Risk Management Framework and Cyber Resilience Framework, advising on critical technology projects and escalating issues that could affect the institution's risk tolerance, and providing independent views to the board and senior management on third-party assessments where necessary.
evidence: Risk treatment plans with assigned owners
Risk acceptance approvals at appropriate authority level

artifacts.name: Risk Treatment Policy
artifacts.description: Policy defining risk treatment options and approval authorities

artifacts.name: Risk Treatment Plan Register
artifacts.description: Register of all risk treatment plans with owners, timelines, and status

artifacts.name: Risk Acceptance Records
artifacts.description: Formal risk acceptance approvals signed by appropriate authority

title: Designation of Chief Information Security Officer
verbatim: A financial institution must designate a Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) by whatever name called, to be responsible for the technology risk management function of the financial institution. The financial institution must ensure that the CISO has sufficient authority, independence and resources. The CISO shall– (a) be independent from day-to-day technology operations; (b) apprise board and senior management of current and emerging technology risks which could potentially affect the financial institution’s risk profile; (c) have the requisite technical skills in emerging and core technologies used by the institution, expertise and experience in audit, governance and risk management, strategic planning and execution of IT and cybersecurity programs, and third party risk management; and (d) be appropriately certified.
translation: A financial institution must designate a Chief Information Security Officer who has sufficient authority, independence, and resources, and who is independent from day-to-day technology operations. The CISO must keep the board and senior management informed of current and emerging technology risks, possess requisite technical skills and expertise in governance, risk management, strategic planning, and third-party risk management, and hold appropriate professional certifications.
evidence: Key risk indicator definitions and thresholds

artifacts.name: Risk Monitoring Procedures
artifacts.description: Procedures for continuous monitoring and reporting of technology risks

artifacts.name: KRI Monitoring Dashboard
artifacts.description: Dashboard or report showing key risk indicators and their status

artifacts.name: Escalation Procedures
artifacts.description: Documented escalation procedures for risk appetite threshold breaches

title: CISO Responsibilities for Information Protection
verbatim: The CISO shall be responsible for ensuring that the financial institution’s information assets and technologies are adequately protected, which includes– (a) formulating appropriate policies for the effective implementation of TRMF and CRF; (b) enforcing compliance with these policies, frameworks and other technology-related regulatory requirements; and (c) advising senior management on technology risk and security matters, including developments in the financial institution’s technology security risk profile in relation to its business and operations.
translation: The CISO is responsible for ensuring the institution's information assets and technologies are adequately protected. This includes formulating policies for effective implementation of the Technology Risk Management Framework and Cyber Resilience Framework, enforcing compliance with those policies and relevant regulatory requirements, and advising senior management on technology risk and security matters including developments in the institution's technology security risk profile.
evidence: Technology risk register with all required fields
Quarterly review records of the risk register

artifacts.name: Technology Risk Register
artifacts.description: Comprehensive register of all identified technology risks with assessments and treatment plans

artifacts.name: Quarterly Review Records
artifacts.description: Evidence of quarterly review and update of the risk register

artifacts.name: Risk Owner Sign-off Records
artifacts.description: Records of risk owners acknowledging their assigned risks
